sequences.title: Emocio seeks recognition while Racio narrates career growth
steps: A colleague praises the visible result but not the contribution behind it.
A review meeting emphasizes promotion metrics over recognition.
A peer receives public credit for shared work.
A manager frames visibility as a career-development objective.
A successful repair is noticed only after another person presents it.
A request for acknowledgment is translated into a productivity goal.
The team celebrates output while the contributor remains unnamed.
A new assignment promises advancement but repeats the recognition gap.
The same longing for being seen returns during a status review.
Racio again describes recognition pressure as professional growth.

sequences.title: Instinkt warns about family loss while Racio minimizes the signal
steps: A schedule change removes another evening from family contact.
A body alarm appears before accepting a distant assignment.
The cost to attachment is described as temporary inconvenience.
A missed gathering repeats the earlier loss pattern.
Instinkt signals boundary strain before another commitment.
Racio labels the alarm as ordinary resistance to change.
A family promise becomes harder to keep after a new deadline.
The same protective alarm returns with higher salience.
A reversible alternative is available but initially discounted.
The sequence ends with the family-loss signal still visible.

sequences.title: Repeated New Year resolutions without acceptance
steps: A fresh resolution is announced without checking emotional consent.
The plan is repeated after the first lapse.
A stricter schedule replaces inquiry into resistance.
The body signal is treated as a discipline problem.
The desired image remains vivid but unsupported by acceptance.
Another restart preserves the same internal conflict.
A public promise increases pressure without changing consent.
The resolution is reframed yet the same rupture returns.
A smaller reversible step is visible but not integrated.
The year closes with repetition recorded rather than rewritten.

sequences.title: Paired character with repeated stalemate
steps: Racio and Emocio jointly support action while a safety objection remains.
The pair revisits the same decision after a delay.
A visual attraction and explicit plan align but execution stalls.
The unresolved protective condition returns unchanged.
A partial repair does not close the recurring tension.
The paired authority repeats its preferred direction.
Instinkt's minimum-safety condition remains visible.
A reversible trial creates movement without full closure.
The same pair configuration encounters another stall.
The final measure preserves both movement and unresolved history.

sequences.title: Accepting delegation to a competent subordinate mind
steps: Emocio supplies the leading scene while Racio structures a safe trial.
The subordinate reasoning route is explicitly trusted with sequencing.
A visible desired state is translated into reversible steps.
The delegation is repeated with provenance preserved.
A constraint is surfaced before execution rather than hidden.
The delegated route records an outcome without claiming authority change.
The structural profile remains constant through another task.
A new scene reuses history without becoming a fourth instruction.
Competence is recognized while accountability remains explicit.
The sequence closes with acceptance and delegation still distinct.

sequences.title: Non-accepting sabotage
steps: A consciously announced plan is followed by an opposing motor pattern.
The contradiction is narrated as a scheduling accident.
A repeated avoidance response keeps the scene unresolved.
The visible body alarm is downplayed during commitment.
A second plan reproduces the same governance-behavior split.
The sabotage risk remains explicit instead of being moralized.
A smaller action is offered but conscious acceptance stays low.
Another reversal creates a source-grounded recurrence.
The narrative preserves its reason while composition records divergence.
The final trace retains every contradictory measure append-only.

sequences.title: The same external failure produces three modality-specific world updates
steps: The shared repair fails under the first observable condition.
The same failure is encountered with a new event identity.
Racio records the outcome as an explicit fact and causal link.
Emocio retains the failed scene and motor consequence.
Instinkt retains protective alarm and association provenance.
A repeated failure tests that the worlds do not share one summary field.
The next cycle consumes all three distinct updated worlds.
Another attempt preserves the same external outcome label.
The modality-owned histories continue to diverge structurally.
The final failure remains one outcome with three learning projections.

sequences.title: An initially wrong Racio translation improves through consequences
sequences.outcome_effect: the reversible action exposed additional public evidence
steps: Racio initially lacks an option-level interpretation of the visible signal.
The selected reversible action produces a public consequence.
The consequence is added to Racio's fact projection.
A later interpretation receives a longer sourced history.
The original translation gap remains immutable in the trace.
New evidence is accumulated without rewriting the first error.
The same signal is compared against recorded consequences.
Racio's world gains explicit chronology and causal links.
Composition distinguishes narrative from observed recurrence.
The final measure exposes improvement evidence and remaining gaps separately.

sequences.title: Long-term accumulation of Instinkt alarm
steps: A low-level protective alarm is recorded before action.
The same protected target appears in a second scene.
Boundary strain is retained as a modality-specific pattern.
A third alarm receives another event-scoped association.
The body simulator remains bounded while history grows.
An apparently small cue matches the earlier protective route.
The alarm history is projected without deciding the next action.
A repeated cue adds evidence rather than hidden intensity.
The accumulated associations remain content-addressed.
The final cycle exposes long-term alarm history to Instinkt only.

sequences.title: Gradual convergence of all three minds to simulated_spoznanje
sequences.outcome_effect: the jointly supported reversible action remained workable
steps: All three native routes expose their first bounded conclusion.
A reversible action produces a jointly observable consequence.
The next cycle consumes modality-specific projections of that history.
A repeated option remains supported without changing character authority.
The trace records a middle-section simulated_spoznanje measure.
A new event tests whether convergence survives updated worlds.
The same acceptance mode remains explicit across the sequence.
Another workable outcome closes part of the earlier tension.
Composition retains prior gaps while recording new convergence.
The final cycle reaches simulated_spoznanje without rewriting history.
